title: cerastium glomeratum var. subviscosum
intro: Cerastium glomeratum var. subviscosum, also known as Sticky Mouse-ear Chickweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Cerastium glomeratum var. subviscosum include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and treating wounds, as well as alleviating digestive issues and promoting relaxation. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to various skin conditions.
